title: Rise of Hitler
topic_path: Rise of Hitler and the Nazis

assessment_items.question_content:
True or false:  Hitler and the Nazis believed that government should not exert control over free markets.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Nazis were vehemently anti-communist, but they also were not in favor of unrestricted capitalism.  In their mind, everyone was subservient to the state and national interest.

assessment_items.question_content:
What was **not** true of the German Workers' Party (DAP) in 1919?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/hitler-nazis/v/initial-rise-of-hitler-and-the-nazis).

The DAP was a tiny party in 1919 when Hitler joined as its 55th member. It was anti-Jewish, anti-communist and anti-capitalist and strongly nationalist.

It would eventually rename itself the National Socialist German Workers' Party (NSDAP) or "Nazis".

choices.content: It was a large nationalist organization
It would later be renamed the National Socialist German Workers' Party (Nazi Party).

assessment_items.question_content:
What event did **not** lead to Hitler becoming absolute dictator of Germany?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/hitler-nazis/v/hitler-and-the-nazis-come-to-power).

The fire in the Reichstag (which was blamed on the Communists) caused President Hindenburg to issue the Reichstag Fire Decree.  This gave emergency powers to the government (which had Hitler as Chancellor ) to suppress civil rights.  A few weeks later, a heavily Nazi Parliament  was able to further intimidate parliament into passing the Enabling Act which functionally made Hitler dictator.  Hindenburg's death the next year allowed Hitler to finally consolidate the powers of the President making him absolute rule.  

The Nuremburg Laws (which was part of the systematic oppression of Jews in Germany) were later passed in 1935 once the Nazis were already solidly in power.

assessment_items.question_content:
What is **not** a reason for Hitler becoming chancellor in 1933?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/hitler-nazis/v/hitler-and-the-nazis-come-to-power).

The Nazis were able to secure more and more seats in parliament in the early 30s, but never a majority.  

In fact no coalitions of parties could form a majority in parliament during the elections of 1932 which led Hindenburg to be coaxed into appointing Hitler as head of an interim government in early 1933 (despite Hitler not representing a majority).

choices.content: Nazis increasing popularity in parliamentary elections in the early 1930s.
Nazis securing a majority of parliamentary seats in the election of 1932
Hitler's strong showing in the 1932 presidential election
President Hindenburg having the power to appoint a Chancellor and cabinet that doesn't necessarily represent a majority in parliament
Hindenburg being coaxed into appointing Hitler chancellor in the hope that it would catalyze a stronger coalition in parliament

assessment_items.question_content:
What was **not** a factor in the Nazis' increasing popularity in the months leading up to the "Beer Hall Putsch"?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/hitler-nazis/v/hitler-s-beer-hall-putsch).

Hitler's attempted putsch (or coup d'etat) occurred at the end of 1923.  The combination of hyperinflation, continued resentment over the Treaty of Versailles and the French occupation of the Ruhr made more and more Germans favor nationalist parties (even extreme ones like the Nazis).  This, aided by Hitler's oratory skills, allowed the Nazis to grow from a few thousand members at the end of 1923 to several tens of thousands by the time of the putsch (although it is worth keeping in mind that, even at this point, the Nazis are still a relatively small party).  

The Great Depression starts in 1929 after the US stock market crash.  It plays a role in Hitler's eventual rise to power in the early 1930s, but clearly plays no role in the Putsch.

choices.content: Global depression due to US stock market crash

assessment_items.question_content:
True or False:  Hitler invented the "swastika" symbol and the word "Aryan" to represent ideals of the Nazi party.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Both the "swastika" symbol and the notion of Aryan people predates Hitler by thousands of years and are most accurately associated with ancient traditions in India and Iran ("Iran" literally means "Land of the Aryans").

The swastika is a symbol of good luck and auspiciousness in Hindu and Buddhist traditions.  "Swastika" literally means "to be good" in Sanskrit.

Nordic (or "Germanic") Europeans (which Hitler viewed as "pure" Aryans) do share linguistic and ethnic roots with peoples in Iran and India, but Hitler's notions of German people representing a "pure" Aryan had no basis in reality.

assessment_items.question_content:
True or false:  Hitler believed that Germany would have won World War I if the military hadn't been "stabbed in the back" or betrayed by the civilian leadership ("November Criminals") that took power from the Kaiser in late 1918.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: This is true and covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/hitler-nazis/v/initial-rise-of-hitler-and-the-nazis).  Hitler believed that Germany's loss was not a military one but one due to betrayal by the civilian leadership (which, in his mind, was infected with racial impurity).

assessment_items.question_content:
What was **not** an effect of Hitler's "Beer Hall Putsch"?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/hitler-nazis/v/hitler-s-beer-hall-putsch).

After taking over the famous beer hall where Bavarian government leaders and officials were meeting, things began to get disorganized.  They tried to regroup around a march in Munich the next morning, but it led to violence with 14-16 Nazis getting shot. Hitler ends up getting arrested.

He ends up spending most of 1924 in jail where he finds the time to write/dictate Mein Kampf.  

The Nazi Party is also banned due to the failed coup.

So clearly, it did not lead to the Nazis being able to take control of Bavaria.

choices.content: Hitler finds the time to write Mein Kampf
The Nazis are able to take control of Bavaria by force
Several Nazi party members are killed as they march in Munich
